speaker: BERTRAM
text: They told me that your name was Fontibell.

speaker: DIANA
text: No, my good lord, Diana.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Titled goddess,

speaker: BERTRAM
text: And worth it, with addition. But, fair soul,

speaker: BERTRAM
text: In your fine frame hath love no quality?

speaker: BERTRAM
text: If the quick fire of youth light not your mind,

speaker: BERTRAM
text: You are no maiden but a monument.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: When you are dead, you should be such a one

speaker: BERTRAM
text: As you are now, for you are cold and stern,

speaker: BERTRAM
text: And now you should be as your mother was

speaker: BERTRAM
text: When your sweet self was got.

speaker: DIANA
text: She then was honest.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: So should you be.

speaker: DIANA
text: No.

speaker: DIANA
text: My mother did but duty—such, my lord,

speaker: DIANA
text: As you owe to your wife.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: No more o’ that.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: I prithee do not strive against my vows.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: I was compelled to her, but I love thee

speaker: BERTRAM
text: By love’s own sweet constraint, and will forever

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Do thee all rights of service.

speaker: DIANA
text: Ay, so you serve us

speaker: DIANA
text: Till we serve you. But when you have our roses,

speaker: DIANA
text: You barely leave our thorns to prick ourselves

speaker: DIANA
text: And mock us with our bareness.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: How have I sworn!

speaker: DIANA
text: ’Tis not the many oaths that makes the truth,

speaker: DIANA
text: But the plain single vow that is vowed true.

speaker: DIANA
text: What is not holy, that we swear not by,

speaker: DIANA
text: But take the high’st to witness. Then pray you, tell me,

speaker: DIANA
text: If I should swear by Jove’s great attributes

speaker: DIANA
text: I loved you dearly, would you believe my oaths

speaker: DIANA
text: When I did love you ill? This has no holding

speaker: DIANA
text: To swear by him whom I protest to love

speaker: DIANA
text: That I will work against him. Therefore your oaths

speaker: DIANA
text: Are words, and poor conditions but unsealed,

speaker: DIANA
text: At least in my opinion.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Change it, change it.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Be not so holy-cruel. Love is holy,

speaker: BERTRAM
text: And my integrity ne’er knew the crafts

speaker: BERTRAM
text: That you do charge men with. Stand no more off,

speaker: BERTRAM
text: But give thyself unto my sick desires,

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Who then recovers. Say thou art mine, and ever

speaker: BERTRAM
text: My love as it begins shall so persever.

speaker: DIANA
text: I see that men may rope ’s in such a snare

speaker: DIANA
text: That we’ll forsake ourselves. Give me that ring.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: I’ll lend it thee, my dear, but have no power

speaker: BERTRAM
text: To give it from me.

speaker: DIANA
text: Will you not, my lord?

speaker: BERTRAM
text: It is an honor ’longing to our house,

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Bequeathèd down from many ancestors,

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Which were the greatest obloquy i’ th’ world

speaker: BERTRAM
text: In me to lose.

speaker: DIANA
text: Mine honor’s such a ring.

speaker: DIANA
text: My chastity’s the jewel of our house,

speaker: DIANA
text: Bequeathèd down from many ancestors,

speaker: DIANA
text: Which were the greatest obloquy i’ th’ world

speaker: DIANA
text: In me to lose. Thus your own proper wisdom

speaker: DIANA
text: Brings in the champion Honor on my part

speaker: DIANA
text: Against your vain assault.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Here, take my ring.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: My house, mine honor, yea, my life be thine,

speaker: BERTRAM
text: And I’ll be bid by thee.

speaker: DIANA
text: When midnight comes, knock at my chamber window.

speaker: DIANA
text: I’ll order take my mother shall not hear.

speaker: DIANA
text: Now will I charge you in the band of truth,

speaker: DIANA
text: When you have conquered my yet maiden bed,

speaker: DIANA
text: Remain there but an hour, nor speak to me.

speaker: DIANA
text: My reasons are most strong, and you shall know them

speaker: DIANA
text: When back again this ring shall be delivered.

speaker: DIANA
text: And on your finger in the night I’ll put

speaker: DIANA
text: Another ring, that what in time proceeds

speaker: DIANA
text: May token to the future our past deeds.

speaker: DIANA
text: Adieu till then; then, fail not. You have won

speaker: DIANA
text: A wife of me, though there my hope be done.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: A heaven on Earth I have won by wooing thee.

speaker: DIANA
text: For which live long to thank both heaven and me!

speaker: DIANA
text: You may so in the end.

speaker: DIANA
text: My mother told me just how he would woo

speaker: DIANA
text: As if she sat in ’s heart. She says all men

speaker: DIANA
text: Have the like oaths. He had sworn to marry me

speaker: DIANA
text: When his wife’s dead. Therefore I’ll lie with him

speaker: DIANA
text: When I am buried. Since Frenchmen are so braid,

speaker: DIANA
text: Marry that will, I live and die a maid.

speaker: DIANA
text: Only, in this disguise I think ’t no sin

speaker: DIANA
text: To cozen him that would unjustly win.
